mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS over the prior 3 months lowers discount rates and supports equity valuation expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during periods where FEDFUNDS is flat or falling for 3 months, or if the signal turns negative when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or declining.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals weakening household income and demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the threshold is crossed.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return after the unemployment threshold is crossed is not materially below unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both improve together, the economy is showing synchronized consumer demand and housing activity, which supports earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and rising HOUST does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in the majority of joint-improvement episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after a prior contraction signals improving industrial supply and cyclical activity, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings-risk perceptions ease.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month after a previous monthly decline
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive after INDPRO inflects upward, or if the effect disappears in repeated rebound episodes across rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: A high policy rate regime with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% tightens financial conditions and compresses equity multiples, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if returns after high-rate triggers are not below average or if SPX still posts positive 20-day returns in the majority of episodes with FEDFUNDS above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth is strong while UNRATE is falling, labor income and spending capacity improve together, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to outperform single-variable labor signals, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in most matched episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in GDPC1 indicates broad real activity improvement that should translate into higher corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP upswings are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the relation vanishes in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-disinflation regime, stable or falling CPIAUCSL together with steady RSAFS implies improving real consumer purchasing power and demand, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of flat-to-falling CPIAUCSL and stable-to-rising RSAFS does not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than baseline, or if inflation-stable periods still show negative average returns.
